title: Using the phscs Package
text: can install development version phscs GitHub using pak package:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("yng-me/phscs")

title: Using the phscs Package
text: One main functions phscs package get_psgc(), retrieves Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) data. function allows specify various parameters version, level geographic data, whether harmonize minimize dataset. can also retrieve data specific levels, regions, provinces, municipalities, barangays. can also filter data using dplyr syntax. example, get areas region code “10”: can also retrieve full PSGC dataset available columns setting minimal = FALSE specifying additional columns include:
code: # Retrieve PSGC data psgc_data <- get_psgc()  psgc_data |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") psgc_provinces <- get_psgc(level = "provinces") psgc_regions <- get_psgc(level = "regions")  psgc_regions |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") # Retrieve PSGC data for areas in region 10 psgc_region_10 <- get_psgc(grepl("^10", area_code))  psgc_region_10 |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") # Retrieve full PSGC data psgc_full_data <- get_psgc(minimal = FALSE)  # Retrieve PSGC data with additional columns psgc_data_with_cols <- get_psgc(   cols = c(     "area_code",      "area_name",      "region_code",      "province_code"   ) )  psgc_data_with_cols |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Using the phscs Package
text: get_psic() function retrieves Philippine Standard Industrial Classification (PSIC) data. can specify level detail want, sections divisions.
code: # Retrieve PSIC data psic_data <- get_psic()  # Retrieve PSIC data for specific levels psic_sections <- get_psic(level = "sections")  psic_sections |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Using the phscs Package
text: get_psoc() function retrieves Philippine Standard Occupational Classification (PSOC) data. Similar PSIC, can specify level detail want.
code: # Retrieve PSOC data psoc_data <- get_psoc()  # Retrieve PSOC data for specific levels psoc_major <- get_psoc(level = "major")  psoc_major |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Using the phscs Package
text: get_psced() function retrieves Philippine Standard Classification Education (PSCED) data. can specify level detail want, broadfield narrowfield. can also retrieve data specific levels, broadfield narrowfield:
code: # Retrieve PSCED data psced_data <- get_psced() psced_data |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") # Retrieve PSCED data for specific levels psced_broadfield <- get_psced(level = "broadfield")  psced_broadfield |>    head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

what: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP)
title: Using the phscs Package
text: get_pcoicop() function retrieves Philippine Classification Individual Consumption According Purpose (PCOICOP) data. can specify level detail want, divisions groups.
code: # Retrieve PCOICOP data pcoicop_data <- get_pcoicop() pcoicop_data |>   head(10) |>    gt::gt() |>    gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bhas Abdulsamad. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdulsamad B (2025). phscs: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems. R package version 0.1.0, https://yng-.github.io/phscs/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {phscs: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems},   author = {Bhas Abdulsamad},   year = {2025},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://yng-me.github.io/phscs/}, }

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
text: goal phscs provide unified interface access manipulate various Philippine statistical classifications, Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC), Philippine Standard Industrial Classification (PSIC), Philippine Standard Occupational Classification (PSOC), others. allows users retrieve, filter, harmonize classification data Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)’s open-access data sources, making easier work Philippine statistical data R.

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
text: Install CRAN: can also install development version phscs GitHub using pak package:
code: install.packages("phscs") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("yng-me/phscs")

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
code: library(phscs)  # Retrieve Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) data psgc_data <- get_psgc() head(psgc_data)  # Retrieve Philippine Standard Industrial Classification (PSIC) data psic_data <- get_psic() head(psic_data)  # Retrieve Philippine Standard Occupational Classification (PSOC) data psoc_data <- get_psoc() head(psoc_data)

what: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: Philippine Classification Individual Consumption According Purpose (PCOICOP)

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
code: get_pcoicop(   ...,   token = NULL,   version = NULL,   level = NULL,   harmonize = TRUE,   minimal = TRUE,   cols = NULL )

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: ... See ?dplyr::filter. Expressions return logical value, defined terms variables returned data. multiple expressions included, combined & operator. rows conditions evaluate TRUE kept. token Character. API access token. version Character. Version PCOICOP retrieve. Default NULL. NULL, latest version used. level Character. Level detail retrieve. Options "", "divisions", "groups", "class", "sub-class", "item", "subitem". harmonize Logical. TRUE, formats standardizes returned data. Default TRUE. minimal Logical. TRUE, returns simplified dataset. Default TRUE. cols Optional. Character vector additional columns include minimal = FALSE.

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ get_pcoicop(token = "your_api_token") } # } # If token is not provided, the function will fetch from local cache or # download the latest version from remote repo pcoicop <- get_pcoicop()  # Get specific level pcoicop_filtered <- get_pcoicop(level = "divisions") pcoicop_filtered #> # A tibble: 15 × 2 #>    value label                                                                   #>    <chr> <chr>                                                                   #>  1 01    Food and non-alcoholic beverages                                        #>  2 02    Alcoholic beverages, tobacco and narcotics                              #>  3 03    Clothing and footwear                                                   #>  4 04    Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels                        #>  5 05    Furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance      #>  6 06    Health                                                                  #>  7 07    Transport                                                               #>  8 08    Information and communication                                           #>  9 09    Recreation, sport and culture                                           #> 10 10    Education services                                                      #> 11 11    Restaurants and accommodation services                                  #> 12 12    Insurance and financial services                                        #> 13 13    Personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services   #> 14 14    Individual consumption expenditure of non-profit institutions serving … #> 15 15    Individual consumption expenditure of general government

title: Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) — get_psgc
text: ... See ?dplyr::filter. Expressions return logical value, defined terms variables returned data. multiple expressions included, combined & operator. rows conditions evaluate TRUE kept. token Character. API access token. NULL, retrieves data local cache. version Character. PSGC version : "Q3_2025", "July_2025", "Q2_2025", "Q1_2025", "April_2024", "Q4_2024", "Q3_2024", "Q2_2024", "Q4_2023", "Q2_2021". NULL, retrieves latest version available local cache. level Character. Level geographic data retrieve. Available options : "", "regions", "provinces", "hucs" "municipalities", "sub_municipalities" "barangays", "income_classification", "urban_rural", "city_class". harmonize Logical. TRUE, formats standardizes returned data. Default TRUE. minimal Logical. TRUE, returns simplified dataset. Default TRUE. cols Optional. Character vector additional columns include minimal = FALSE.

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: function shortens region names PSGC data frame.

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
code: shorten_region_name(data, which = c("label", "number"), col = "area_name")

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: data data frame containing PSGC data. Character. Specifies whether shorten region name label number. Options "label" "number". col Character. name column containing area names. Default "area_name".

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: data frame region names shortened based specified argument.
